response.bodyRaw:
{"status":"failed","steps":{"input":{"question":"Is there a relationship between course credits and average grade?"},"sql":{"payload":{"question":"Is there a relationship between course credits and average grade?"},"startedAt":1771804207114,"status":"success","output":{"reasoning":"Compute per-student numeric score from available numeric grade components, aggregate to per-course average, then report overall correlation between course credits and those per-course average grades (Pearson). Excludes grades with no numeric components and ignores non-numeric grade_value. Assumptions listed.","sql":"WITH student_scores AS (
  SELECT
    c.id AS course_id,
    c.credits AS credits,
    g.id AS grade_id,
    (
      (COALESCE(g.exam_score, 0) + COALESCE(g.project_score, 0) + COALESCE(g.attendance_score, 0))
      / NULLIF(
          (CASE WHEN g.exam_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
          + (CASE WHEN g.project_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
          + (CASE WHEN g.attendance_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
        , 0)
    )::numeric AS student_score
  FROM public.courses c
  JOIN public.course_sections cs ON cs.course_id = c.id
  JOIN public.enrollments e ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
  JOIN public.grades g ON g.enrollment_id = e.id
  WHERE (
    (CASE WHEN g.exam_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
    + (CASE WHEN g.project_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
    + (CASE WHEN g.attendance_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
  ) > 0
),
per_course AS (
  SELECT
    course_id,
    credits,
    AVG(student_score) AS avg_grade,
    STDDEV_POP(student_score) AS stddev_grade,
    COUNT(*) AS n_grades
  FROM student_scores
  GROUP BY course_id, credits
)
SELECT
  COUNT(*) AS courses_with_numeric_grades,
  AVG(avg_grade) AS overall_avg_grade,
  STDDEV_POP(avg_grade) AS overall_stddev_avg_grade,
  CORR(avg_grade, credits) AS pearson_corr_credits_avg_grade,
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM public.courses) AS total_courses
FROM per_course;","assumptions":["Numeric relationship is measured using available numeric components (exam_score, project_score, attendance_score); letter/enum grade_value is ignored.","Per-student score is the mean of the non-null numeric components for that student in the course.","Courses with no numeric-grade-bearing enrollments are excluded from the correlation calculation.","If you prefer using grade_value (letters) or different weighting, provide mapping or weights and I will adjust the query."]},"endedAt":1771804254134},"run-raw-sql":{"payload":{"sql":"WITH student_scores AS (

input.question: Is there a relationship between course credits and average grade?

payload.question: Is there a relationship between course credits and average grade?

output.reasoning: Compute per-student numeric score from available numeric grade components, aggregate to per-course average, then report overall correlation between course credits and those per-course average grades (Pearson). Excludes grades with no numeric components and ignores non-numeric grade_value. Assumptions listed.
output.sql:
WITH student_scores AS (
  SELECT
    c.id AS course_id,
    c.credits AS credits,
    g.id AS grade_id,
    (
      (COALESCE(g.exam_score, 0) + COALESCE(g.project_score, 0) + COALESCE(g.attendance_score, 0))
      / NULLIF(
          (CASE WHEN g.exam_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
          + (CASE WHEN g.project_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
          + (CASE WHEN g.attendance_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
        , 0)
    )::numeric AS student_score
  FROM public.courses c
  JOIN public.course_sections cs ON cs.course_id = c.id
  JOIN public.enrollments e ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
  JOIN public.grades g ON g.enrollment_id = e.id
  WHERE (
    (CASE WHEN g.exam_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
    + (CASE WHEN g.project_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
    + (CASE WHEN g.attendance_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
  ) > 0
),
per_course AS (
  SELECT
    course_id,
    credits,
    AVG(student_score) AS avg_grade,
    STDDEV_POP(student_score) AS stddev_grade,
    COUNT(*) AS n_grades
  FROM student_scores
  GROUP BY course_id, credits
)
SELECT
  COUNT(*) AS courses_with_numeric_grades,
  AVG(avg_grade) AS overall_avg_grade,
  STDDEV_POP(avg_grade) AS overall_stddev_avg_grade,
  CORR(avg_grade, credits) AS pearson_corr_credits_avg_grade,
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM public.courses) AS total_courses
FROM per_course;
assumptions: Numeric relationship is measured using available numeric components (exam_score, project_score, attendance_score); letter/enum grade_value is ignored.
Per-student score is the mean of the non-null numeric components for that student in the course.
Courses with no numeric-grade-bearing enrollments are excluded from the correlation calculation.
If you prefer using grade_value (letters) or different weighting, provide mapping or weights and I will adjust the query.

payload.sql:
WITH student_scores AS (
  SELECT
    c.id AS course_id,
    c.credits AS credits,
    g.id AS grade_id,
    (
      (COALESCE(g.exam_score, 0) + COALESCE(g.project_score, 0) + COALESCE(g.attendance_score, 0))
      / NULLIF(
          (CASE WHEN g.exam_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
          + (CASE WHEN g.project_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
          + (CASE WHEN g.attendance_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
        , 0)
    )::numeric AS student_score
  FROM public.courses c
  JOIN public.course_sections cs ON cs.course_id = c.id
  JOIN public.enrollments e ON e.course_section_id = cs.id
  JOIN public.grades g ON g.enrollment_id = e.id
  WHERE (
    (CASE WHEN g.exam_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
    + (CASE WHEN g.project_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
    + (CASE WHEN g.attendance_score IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)
  ) > 0
),
per_course AS (
  SELECT
    course_id,
    credits,
    AVG(student_score) AS avg_grade,
    STDDEV_POP(student_score) AS stddev_grade,
    COUNT(*) AS n_grades
  FROM student_scores
  GROUP BY course_id, credits
)
SELECT
  COUNT(*) AS courses_with_numeric_grades,
  AVG(avg_grade) AS overall_avg_grade,
  STDDEV_POP(avg_grade) AS overall_stddev_avg_grade,
  CORR(avg_grade, credits) AS pearson_corr_credits_avg_grade,
  (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM public.courses) AS total_courses
FROM per_course;
